diagnostic_question: Can the task be decomposed into discrete, independently verifiable checkpoints with unambiguous outcomes?
spider_evidence: Spider SQL components (keywords, tables, JOINs, predicates, aggregates, columns) are each independently checkable via automated verifiers against gold queries
violation_consequence: Without discrete checkpoints, no frequency table can be constructed; ordinal verification-entropy is undefined

diagnostic_question: Does a publicly available, expert-validated rule set exist against which checkpoint outcomes can be checked?
spider_evidence: Spider dataset provides gold queries, schema definitions, and foreign key constraints; SQL-92 standard provides grammar; 12 verifier rules documented in verifier_rule_inventory.csv
violation_consequence: Without external rules, verification becomes circular or subjective; inter-annotator agreement collapses

diagnostic_question: Can checkpoint failure types be ordered along a substantively meaningful severity or complexity gradient?
spider_evidence: Error types order as: correct < minor_keyword_error < column_mismatch < structural_error < execution_failure; ordinal ranking validated by expert annotators
violation_consequence: Without ordinal structure, verification-entropy cannot be interpreted as an ordinal measure; cardinal operations become invalid

diagnostic_question: Are checkpoint outcomes observable after generation completes without access to internal model states?
spider_evidence: All Spider checkpoints are evaluated on generated SQL text only; no access to model activations, attention maps, or generation trajectory required
violation_consequence: Without post-hoc availability, protocol cannot be applied to black-box models or standard benchmark settings
